b' The Lauder Brood Feud, A Surprise Cartier Exit & T-Swift\xe2\x80\x99s Frumpy Ride
Hi, and welcome back to Line Sheet. Today, beauty queen Rachel \xe2\x80\x9cRachel Strugatz is back with intel on the leak of Est\xc3\xa9e Lauder heiress Jane Lauder\xe2\x80\x99s savage letter calling for the removal of her cousin William from the company\xe2\x80\x99s board of directors. Now that St\xc3\xa9phane de La Faverie is slated to take over as C.E.O., will Jane move into William\xe2\x80\x99s executive chairman seat? Rachel has some thoughts. Up top, I\xe2\x80\x99m keeping it light, with Richemont and LVMH H.R. updates and notes on a sneaker sea change. \xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c

Hi, and welcome back to Line Sheet. I\xe2\x80\x99m writing to you from a screening room in Beverly Hills, where I just previewed Queer , the new film by Luca Guadagnino . I\xe2\x80\x99ll be interviewing Queer \xe2\x80\x99s costume designer, Jonathan W. Anderson ( yep, that one ), on Friday at Stories of the Season\xe2\x80\x94Puck\xe2\x80\x99s fancy to-do celebrating the past year in movies\xe2\x80\x94alongside Colleen Atwood ( Beetlejuice Beetlejuice ), Janty Yates ( Gladiator II ), and Virginie Montel ( Emilia P\xc3\xa9rez ). And I\xe2\x80\x99m just kicking things off: What I\xe2\x80\x99m Hearing author Matt Belloni will be chatting with Dune director Denis Villeneuve and Anora star Mikey Madison (!!!) for a live taping of his pod, The Town .

Other big voices slated to speak include documentary heavyweights Matt Tyrnauer , Shiori Ito , Josh Greenbaum , and Morgan Neville , and revered actors Zoe Salda\xc3\xb1a , Jeremy Strong , and Peter Sarsgaard (a.k.a. Tommy Molto), among others. We have a few seats left. If you are a guild member and a Puck subscriber, email Fritz@puck.news to join us! (And if you\xe2\x80\x99re not a Puck subscriber already\xe2\x80\xa6 for shame.)

Today, beauty queen Rachel \xe2\x80\x9c Rachel@puck.news \xe2\x80\x9d Strugatz is back with intel on the leak of Est\xc3\xa9e Lauder heiress Jane Lauder \xe2\x80\x99s savage letter calling for the removal of her cousin William from the company\xe2\x80\x99s board of directors. Now that St\xc3\xa9phane de La Faverie is slated to take over as C.E.O., will Jane move into William\xe2\x80\x99s executive chairman seat? Rachel has some thoughts. Up top, I\xe2\x80\x99m keeping it light, with Richemont and LVMH H.R. updates and notes on a sneaker sea change.

Before we get started, a bit of feedback : Several of the folks who originally wrote to me admonishing Taylor Swift \xe2\x80\x99s velvet sandals\xe2\x80\x94inexplicably worn with a Vivienne Westwood dress during a dinner outing this past weekend\xe2\x80\x94wanted to clarify that they actually liked the sandals, but simply thought they didn\xe2\x80\x99t look right with the outfit. (I agree, I like the sandals but they didn\xe2\x80\x99t work with the dress.) Kate Young and I discussed this weighty topic on Tuesday\xe2\x80\x99s episode of Fashion People . Anyway, moving on\xe2\x80\xa6

Mentioned in this issue : William Lauder , Jane Lauder , The Est\xc3\xa9e Lauder Companies, St\xc3\xa9phane de La Faverie , Chanel, LVMH, Bernard and Delphine Arnault , Cartier, Sophie Brocart , the sneakers you want now, Chlo\xc3\xa9, Loewe, Chantal Gaemperle , Sidney Toledano , Mercedes Abramo , Michael Burke , and many more\xe2\x80\xa6

A MESSAGE FROM OUR SPONSOR
Flawless glam goes with everything. Book now with code LINESHEET to get 20% off your next Glamsquad service. Terms apply.
Three Things You Should Know\xe2\x80\xa6
  • Richemont\xe2\x80\x99s other loss : Longtime Cartier executive Mercedes Abramo is leaving the business, I\xe2\x80\x99m told. Her last day is Friday.

    For the past year and half, Abramo has been based in Switzerland, overseeing commercial activities across regions, from stores to pricing to distribution. But before that, she was C.E.O. of the North American business, and before that , she ran the Fifth Avenue flagship. A great thing about the retail biz is that you can work your way up from the store to the corporate offices; it doesn\xe2\x80\x99t happen as much as it used to, but it still happens. (A rep for Cartier declined to comment.)

    Anyway, there are whispers about whether Abramo is headed to Tiffany, the LVMH-owned archrival, where many of her colleagues have landed and big changes are expected to be announced in the coming weeks. (Tiffany isn\xe2\x80\x99t yet a threat to Cartier\xe2\x80\x94which is still way nicer\xe2\x80\x94but it\xe2\x80\x99s chipping away.) For what it\xe2\x80\x99s worth, I\xe2\x80\x99ve heard that Abramo\xe2\x80\x99s next move is not set in stone just yet. Good to remember that she was an acolyte of recently departed (and beloved) C.E.O. Cyrille Vigneron , and that she is American. I love Switzerland\xe2\x80\x94 especially in the summer\xe2\x80\x94but I\xe2\x80\x99m not sure I\xe2\x80\x99d want to live there full-time.

  • An LVMH exec scoots over to Chanel : Around the end of Paris Fashion Week, I heard that longtime LVMH executive Sophie Brocart , most recently the C.E.O. of Patou, was out. Now, WWD is reporting that Brocart has landed at Chanel to lead some sort of eco initiative focused on circularity. The first question, of course, is about the viability of Patou\xe2\x80\x94the tiny, LVMH-owned brand that\xe2\x80\x99s designed by Guillaume Henry , one of these youngish talents that people like Bernard Arnault and Sidney Toledano are desperate to keep close. Patou has always been about the fragrance; Brocart and Henry ran it like a startup, so whether or not it continues will depend on how badly LVMH wants to keep Henry on their roster, especially if they have nowhere else to put him.

    I always wondered why Brocart, who spent a lot of time working with Delphine Arnault and other senior executives on recruiting young talent\xe2\x80\x94for the LVMH Prize, but also within the LVMH Fashion Group\xe2\x80\x94wasn\xe2\x80\x99t made C.E.O. of the company\xe2\x80\x99s fashion group when Michael Burke stepped down. As someone on the LVMH board reminded me a couple of weeks ago, the Fashion Group is so, so small compared to Louis Vuitton and Dior\xe2\x80\x94and the executive in charge has far less clout than the people running those brands. That said, the fashion group should be run by someone who is interested in identifying and working closely with young talent, because the smaller brands can serve as a feeder to the larger ones. Former fashion group leader Pierre - Yves Roussel brought in Phoebe Philo , Jonathan Anderson, Riccardo Tisci , and many executives.

    Anyway, perhaps Brocart\xe2\x80\x99s departure has no connection to the departure of ousted H.R. head Chantal Gaemperle , whom I\xe2\x80\x99ll have more on soon. As we all know, this is only the beginning of the changes going down at the second-biggest company in Europe.

  • About this new high-top Chlo\xc3\xa9 sneaker : It\xe2\x80\x99s called the Kick , is inspired by boxing shoes and ballet slippers, and is giving extreme Isabel Marant Beckett vibes\xe2\x80\x94but without the wedge\xe2\x80\x94and also riffing on the Puma Speedcat , further indicating that we have entered a new sneaker cycle. (I expect we\xe2\x80\x99ll see it on the Lyst list a few quarters from now.) One style that will likely carry over in this transition is the Loewe runner . As my friend Claire ( Chris \xe2\x80\x99s wife, actually) texted last week, \xe2\x80\x9cI\xe2\x80\x99ve been stuck at LaGuardia for many many hours and the number of Loewe runners I\xe2\x80\x99ve seen is entirely unprecedented.\xe2\x80\x9d Hard data for you. If you\xe2\x80\x99re interested further in the Chlo\xc3\xa9 influence and why ever hard-hearted ladies like me are cottoning, read Becky Malinsky \xe2\x80\x99s latest missive.
And now, the story that the Lauder family has been fearing for days\xe2\x80\xa6
The Lauder Blood Sacrifice
After being passed over for the C.E.O. job, and as she exits her family\xe2\x80\x99s storied beauty company, Jane Lauder took a sharpened eyeliner pencil to her cousin William in a leaked letter calling for his ouster from the board. Is this a petty family skirmish, an overdue governance change, or the first salvo in a new era at the historic beauty company?
RACHEL STRUGATZ
Another shoe has dropped in the long-running, multigenerational drama of the Lauder family. Last week, The Wall Street Journal reported that first cousins William and Jane are feuding, Exhibit A being a letter Jane sent to certain members of the Est\xc3\xa9e Lauder board blaming William, the chairman, for \xe2\x80\x9cdestroying the company.\xe2\x80\x9d We\xe2\x80\x99ve all heard about the rumored dissension within the family\xe2\x80\x93\xe2\x80\x93from how the company should be run, to who should run it, to where its brands should be sold (Amazon is working out well, by the way)\xe2\x80\x93\xe2\x80\x93but it\xe2\x80\x99s rare that the inner machinery of America\xe2\x80\x99s most storied beauty dynasty is exposed for all to see, and by a member of the family, no less. It\xe2\x80\x99s not pretty.

The proximate cause of the rift was the elevation of St\xc3\xa9phane de La Faverie to C.E.O., replacing the outgoing Fabrizio Freda . It was a job that Jane herself very much coveted and, as both a family member and the company\xe2\x80\x99s chief data officer and executive vice president of enterprise marketing, perhaps regarded as her birthright. When Jane learned that de La Faverie was to be anointed\xe2\x80\x94before the news was made public\xe2\x80\x94she resigned. Around the same time, William, whom Jane blamed for enabling Freda\xe2\x80\x99s long and recently unprofitable tenure, announced he\xe2\x80\x99d be stepping down as executive chairman. Of course, there were nuances here, too: Jane was presumably also pissed that William\xe2\x80\x99s support of Freda had come at the expense of her own elevation. Also, William\xe2\x80\x99s demotion was ostensibly prompted by the board. After all, ELC has endured nine consecutive unprofitable quarters. (A spokesperson for The Est\xc3\xa9e Lauder Companies declined to comment.)

Yes , it\xe2\x80\x99s messy, and yes , Est\xc3\xa9e \xe2\x80\x99s grandkids sound like petulant grown children, and ones who could never contemplate an existence outside the confines of the GM building. But Jane is probably the only family member who\xe2\x80\x99s meaningfully challenged the way ELC operates, and she\xe2\x80\x99s a more formidable opponent than anyone realized. \xe2\x80\x9cIt looks like Jane has some balls,\xe2\x80\x9d said a person with ties to the family. \xe2\x80\x9cShe doesn\xe2\x80\x99t feel that the board has acted as it should have, and William was the executive chairman and said yes , yes , yes to Fabrizio time and again.\xe2\x80\x9d

Freda, unfairly or not, has been shouldering much of the blame for the company\xe2\x80\x99s market cap freefall. He\xe2\x80\x99s been C.E.O. since 2008, and for many of those years, things were very good. The Lauder family, as averse to leadership change as most wealthy families, was hoping that the same executive would be able to turn things around, even after it became clear he couldn\xe2\x80\x99t. William, one of several family members with outsize influence on the board, was likely in favor of de La Faverie\xe2\x80\x99s promotion, much to Jane\xe2\x80\x99s chagrin.

On some level, perhaps, none of this is entirely surprising. William, 64, was the C.E.O. before Freda\xe2\x80\x94until the board forced him to step down in 2008 while he was still in his 40s. (The board created the executive chairman role for him as a consolation prize.) Sixteen years later, some of the very same board members again took away William\xe2\x80\x99s role. Alas, any efforts the family had made to \xe2\x80\x9ctake the heat off William\xe2\x80\x9d and protect him from being tainted by the current mess, according to a source who knows the family, appear to have been in vain.
A MESSAGE FROM OUR SPONSOR
Flawless glam goes with everything. Book now with code LINESHEET to get 20% off your next Glamsquad service. Terms apply.
The Leak
Jane\xe2\x80\x99s letter was clearly a sort of theatrical artifact, enabling her to distance herself from corporate ineptitude, nuke a family member, and put the board on notice while possibly courting the fealty of shareholders. The question of who leaked her manifesto to the Journal is less clear. One theory is that it was Jane\xe2\x80\x99s own team, with the intention to push the board to name her as executive chairperson. The thinking goes that Jane surely knew anything she put in writing could eventually be made public.

A former executive dismissed this scenario. \xe2\x80\x9cIf it was her, we would have been hearing rumblings 10 years ago,\xe2\x80\x9d this person said. \xe2\x80\x9cNobody waits 30 years , and only when they get to wit\xe2\x80\x99s end, and the ultimate humiliation, do they preempt and quit\xe2\x80\x93\xe2\x80\x93on the heels of having done nothing for decades that would ever be called into question, and never having been a source. It\xe2\x80\x99s not her nature.\xe2\x80\x9d (A source close to Jane told me that Jane \xe2\x80\x9cdefinitively didn\xe2\x80\x99t leak it.\xe2\x80\x9d) The Chapin and Stanford grad has always been introverted and cautious, much less visible socially and across the brand than her sister, Aerin .

Jane\xe2\x80\x99s 80-year-old father, Ronald \xe2\x80\x94the prolific art collector, onetime mayoral candidate, and deep-pocketed Republican donor\xe2\x80\x94could have had the motivation to stir things up. He\xe2\x80\x99s presumably upset that his daughter was passed over while his nephew William cooled his heels. And Ronald, I\xe2\x80\x99m told by several sources, has used the media to his advantage in the past.

A more apposite question may be: Whose reputation remains intact after the Journal \xe2\x80\x99s report? \xe2\x80\x9cThe fact that St\xc3\xa9phane wasn\xe2\x80\x99t painted in a negative light is telling,\xe2\x80\x9d mused an analyst from a top bank. Another source noted that an article about the family\xe2\x80\x99s internecine squabbles certainly validates the board\xe2\x80\x99s decision to go outside the family for the company\xe2\x80\x99s next chief executive.
Jane\xe2\x80\x99s Addiction
Does the 51-year-old Jane really want to step into the newly vacant executive chairperson role after knifing her cousin? My understanding is that some executives and family members believe a more visible role for Jane would help the family retain control. \xe2\x80\x9cThe idea is that if Jane can replace William, she\xe2\x80\x99ll have more power and can act as St\xc3\xa9phane\xe2\x80\x99s boss to better manage steering the ship,\xe2\x80\x9d said a source familiar with the company. A person close to the family disagreed. \xe2\x80\x9cJane was hoping she would get C.E.O., and since she didn\xe2\x80\x99t, she wants to go in and just be a board member where she can add value,\xe2\x80\x9d this person affirmed to me. However, said a person familiar with the situation: \xe2\x80\x9cI don\xe2\x80\x99t think this is over. I don\xe2\x80\x99t think Jane goes from that career to attending four board meetings a year.\xe2\x80\x9d

Either way, I\xe2\x80\x99m hearing that some senior-level employees are very eager for January, when de La Faverie officially starts in his new role. \xe2\x80\x9cThe last couple of years, the focus on Who\xe2\x80\x99s next? or What\xe2\x80\x99s next? has been such a distraction for everyone, and this other drama that\xe2\x80\x99s unfolding is yet another distraction,\xe2\x80\x9d said a current Lauder executive. \xe2\x80\x9cEveryone is over this and ready to move forward. We want to do the things we need to do to reposition this company. There are finally real changes coming.\xe2\x80\x9d
That\xe2\x80\x99s it from Rachel and me. By the way, I\xe2\x80\x99ve been invited to two tarot card readings, sponsored by fashion brands, within the past 24 hours. I don\xe2\x80\x99t know what it means, but it means something .

Until Tomorrow,
Lauren

P.S. : We are using affiliate links because we are a business. We may make a couple bucks off of them.
FOUR STORIES WE\xe2\x80\x99RE TALKING ABOUT
Warners\xe2\x80\x99 I.P. War
The latest twist in the legal feud over Max\xe2\x80\x99s ER reboot.
ERIQ GARDNER
